```json
  "overview": "Diagnose why wall images are rendering with one dominant tint and align the implementation with the Broadsheet mockup’s multi-color card treatment.",
  "plan": "# Fix Multi-Color Event Image Treatment\n\n## What we found\n- The image treatment is implemented in [&#96;/Users/katespurr/httpdocs/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php&#96;](/Users/katespurr/httpdocs/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php) and [&#96;/Users/katespurr/httpdocs/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/assets/css/broadsheet.css&#96;](/Users/katespurr/httpdocs/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/assets/css/broadsheet.css).\n- Current rendering intentionally does &#96;grayscale + single hue tint&#96; via &#96;--hfx-cat-hue&#96;, so if category selection resolves to the same term repeatedly, all cards collapse to one color family.\n- Your note that categories are assigned means the likely issue is category term selection/fallback logic, not missing CSS.\n\n## Implementation plan\n1. **Improve category term selection for color seeding**\n   - Update event payload creation in [&#96;/Users/katespurr/httpdocs/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php&#96;](/Users/katespurr/httpdocs/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php) so hue is derived from the most specific event category term (avoid generic bucket terms when multiple terms exist).\n   - Keep label and slug aligned so display text and hue source come from the same chosen term.\n\n2. **Make hue fallback resistant to single-color collapse**\n   - In the same file, adjust fallback logic so generic/ambiguous category slugs do not all map to the same hue (use a stable per-event seed when needed).\n   - Preserve deterministic output (same event always gets same hue).\n\n3. **Keep existing halftone style but verify mockup parity**\n   - Leave halftone CSS pipeline in [&#96;/Users/katespurr/httpdocs/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/assets/css/broadsheet.css&#96;](/Users/katespurr/httpdocs/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/assets/css/broadsheet.css) intact unless inspection shows a regression.\n   - Confirm image layer behavior still matches prototype intent from [&#96;/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Claude Design/src/shared.jsx&#96;](/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax%20Now/Claude%20Design/src/shared.jsx) (&#96;EventImage&#96; halftone variant).\n\n4. **Verify across templates using the shared image helper**\n   - Validate homepage wall rendering in [&#96;/Users/katespurr/httpdocs/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/front-page.php&#96;](/Users/katespurr/httpdocs/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/front-page.php).\n   - Spot-check any other templates that call &#96;hfx_event_image_e(...)&#96; to ensure color diversity appears consistently.\n\n5. **Acceptance checks**\n   - Home wall shows a visibly mixed palette across cards (not a single repeated tint).\n   - Hue assignment remains stable per event between reloads.\n   - No layout regressions in card image sizing, overlay, or text contrast.",
  "todos": [
    {
      "content": "Refine event category term picking in payload generation to avoid generic repeated hue source.",
      "id": "inspect-term-selection",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Add deterministic per-event fallback to prevent one-color collapse when category keys are generic.",
      "id": "hue-fallback-hardening",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Verify updated color behavior on front-page wall and other helper consumers.",
      "id": "template-verification",
      "status": "pending"
    }
  ]
}
```

Changed `functions.php` in `/Users/katespurr/httpdocs/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet` to fix the single-color collapse:

- Added `hfx_event_category_hue_map()` so category mapping is centralized and reused.
- Added `hfx_event_is_generic_category_slug()` to detect broad buckets like `events`, `uncategorized`, etc.
- Added `hfx_pick_event_category_term()` to choose a better term when multiple categories exist:
  - prefers non-generic terms that have explicit hue mapping
  - otherwise picks first non-generic term
  - falls back to first candidate only if needed
- Updated `hfx_event_to_payload()` to use that term picker instead of always taking the first term.
- Updated hue generation call in payload to include deterministic per-event seed (`post_id`) for generic fallback cases.
- Updated `hfx_event_category_hue()` signature to accept optional `$seed` and use it only when the category key is generic, preserving stable but varied hues.

Verification completed:

- Confirmed all image-render call sites in `front-page.php` and `single-event.php` still pass the computed event hue into `hfx_event_image_e(...)`.
- Confirmed no CSS pipeline changes were made in `assets/css/broadsheet.css` (halftone treatment preserved).
- Ran `ReadLints` on edited/related PHP files: no linter errors.
- Tried CLI PHP linting, but `php` is not available in this shell environment (`command not found`), so syntax verification used editor diagnostics instead.

All existing plan to-dos are now marked `completed`.

import re, requests

vals = re.findall(r'--hfx-cat-hue:\s*([^;\"]+)', html)
u = sorted(set(vals), key=lambda x:int(x.replace('deg','')) if x.endswith('deg') else x)

```bash
print('unique_count', len(u))
print('unique_sample', u[:20])
print('first20', vals[:20])
PY
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: git diff -- "wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php"</summary>

```bash
git diff -- "wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php"
```

```
diff --git a/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php b/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php
index 70dd1bb..2d95ad3 100644
--- a/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php
+++ b/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php
@@ -508,6 +508,27 @@ function hfx_pick_event_category_term( $terms ) {
 	return $candidates[0];
 }
 
+/**
+ * Apply deterministic hue variation so repeated categories do not collapse visually.
+ *
+ * @param int    $hue  Base hue.
+ * @param string $seed Stable event seed.
+ * @return int Degrees 0–359.
+ */
+function hfx_event_apply_hue_variation( $hue, $seed = '' ) {
+	$base_hue = (int) $hue;
+	$base_hue = ( ( $base_hue % 360 ) + 360 ) % 360;
+	$seed     = is_string( $seed ) ? trim( $seed ) : '';
+	if ( '' === $seed ) {
+		return $base_hue;
+	}
+
+	// Offset in roughly [-24, +24] degrees for controlled palette spread.
+	$byte   = hexdec( substr( md5( 'hfx-hue|' . $seed ), 0, 2 ) );
+	$offset = (int) round( ( ( $byte / 255 ) * 48 ) - 24 );
+	return ( $base_hue + $offset + 360 ) % 360;
+}
+
 /**
  * Convert event post to frontend payload (aligns with Halifax ECP spec / JSON contract).
  *
@@ -611,7 +632,8 @@ function hfx_event_to_payload($post_id) {
 
 	$cat_label = $category ? $category : __( 'Events', 'halifax-now-broadsheet' );
 	$cat_key   = $category_slug ? (string) $category_slug : ( $category ? sanitize_title( (string) $category ) : 'events' );
-	$hue       = hfx_event_category_hue( $cat_key, $cat_label, (string) $post_id );
+	$hue_base  = hfx_event_category_hue( $cat_key, $cat_label, (string) $post_id );
+	$hue       = hfx_event_apply_hue_variation( $hue_base, (string) $post_id );
 	$short_acf  = hfx_get_event_field($post_id, 'hfx_short_blurb');
 	$excerpt_p  = (string) get_post_field('post_excerpt', $post_id);
 	$excerpt    = get_the_excerpt($post_id);

```

You were right — I confirmed it still wasn’t behaving as intended, and found why.

What I verified:
- Live homepage was only outputting **2 hue values** (`275deg`, `280deg`) across wall cards, so it looked mostly one color family.
- That means category data is too clustered for the current tint model, even after earlier category selection fixes.

What I changed:
- Added deterministic per-event hue variation in  
  `wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php`
- This keeps category-based color identity, but spreads repeated categories so cards don’t all look the same.

Git status:
- Committed and pushed to your repo `main` successfully.
- Commit: `f902ab2`
- Remote: `https://github.com/katebspurr-png/hfx-now.git`

If the site still looks unchanged now, it’s almost certainly cache/deploy state. Next steps:
1. Pull/deploy latest `main` on the server.
2. Purge page cache/CDN cache (and object cache if enabled).
3. Hard refresh browser (`Cmd+Shift+R`).

If you want, I can also add a temporary debug endpoint/snippet that prints the first 10 wall hue values on-page so we can verify production is serving the new logic immediately.

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 - <<'PY'
import re, requests, json
html = requests.get('https://halifax-now.ca/', timeout=20).text
m = re.search(r'window\.HFX_EVENTS\s*=\s*(\[.*?\]);', html, re.S)
if not m:
    m = re.search(r'id="hfx-events-json"[^>]*>(.*?)</script>', html, re.S)
if m:
    txt=m.group(1)
    print('found payload snippet len',len(txt))
    # try parse array assignment format
    if txt.strip().startswith('['):
        data=json.loads(txt)
    else:
        # maybe window assignment
        arr=re.search(r'(\[.*\])', txt, re.S)
        data=json.loads(arr.group(1)) if arr else []
    print('events',len(data))
    hues=[e.get('hue') for e in data if isinstance(e,dict)]
    print('unique hues',sorted(set(hues))[:30], 'count',len(set(hues)))
    cats={}
    for e in data:
        if not isinstance(e,dict):
            continue
        c=e.get('category') or e.get('categoryLabel')
        cats.setdefault(c,set()).add(e.get('hue'))
    for c,v in list(cats.items())[:10]:
        print(c,sorted(v))
else:
    print('No embedded payload match')
    # fallback extract hue from inline styles
    vals=re.findall(r'--hfx-cat-hue:\s*([^;\"]+)', html)
    print('style unique',sorted(set(vals)),len(set(vals)))
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import re, requests, json
html = requests.get('https://halifax-now.ca/', timeout=20).text
m = re.search(r'window\.HFX_EVENTS\s*=\s*(\[.*?\]);', html, re.S)
if not m:
    m = re.search(r'id="hfx-events-json"[^>]*>(.*?)</script>', html, re.S)
if m:
    txt=m.group(1)
    print('found payload snippet len',len(txt))
    # try parse array assignment format
    if txt.strip().startswith('['):
        data=json.loads(txt)
    else:
        # maybe window assignment
        arr=re.search(r'(\[.*\])', txt, re.S)
        data=json.loads(arr.group(1)) if arr else []
    print('events',len(data))
    hues=[e.get('hue') for e in data if isinstance(e,dict)]
    print('unique hues',sorted(set(hues))[:30], 'count',len(set(hues)))
    cats={}
    for e in data:
        if not isinstance(e,dict):
            continue
        c=e.get('category') or e.get('categoryLabel')
        cats.setdefault(c,set()).add(e.get('hue'))
    for c,v in list(cats.items())[:10]:
        print(c,sorted(v))
else:
    print('No embedded payload match')
    # fallback extract hue from inline styles
    vals=re.findall(r'--hfx-cat-hue:\s*([^;\"]+)', html)
    print('style unique',sorted(set(vals)),len(set(vals)))
PY
```

_**Agent (default)**_

I found the concrete root cause from live data: many events have both `arts-culture` and `live-music`, and the current picker treats `arts-culture` as primary too often. I’m updating term selection priority to prefer specific categories (like `live-music`) over umbrella terms (like `arts-culture`).

# Simulate picker scoring for known problematic term sets
from hashlib import md5

def pr(slug):
    p={
        'live-music':90,'music':90,'comedy':88,'theatre':86,'film':84,'food-drink':82,'food':82,
        'outdoors':80,'community':78,'sports':76,'family':74,'nightlife':72,'market':70,'markets':70,
        'arts-and-culture':20,'arts-culture':20,'arts':20,'events':10,'event':10,
    }
    return p.get(slug,50)

def gen(slug):
    return slug in {'events','event','all-events','general','misc','miscellaneous','other','uncategorized'}

def choose(slugs):
    hue_map=set(['music','live-music','comedy','arts','arts-culture','arts-and-culture','food','food-drink','outdoors','film','theatre','community','sports','family','nightlife','market','markets'])
    best=None; bests=-10**9
    for slug in slugs:
        s=pr(slug)
        if slug in hue_map: s+=20
        if gen(slug): s-=30
        if s>bests:
            bests=s; best=slug
    return best,bests

for terms in [
    ['arts-culture','live-music'],
    ['arts-culture','comedy'],
    ['events','film'],
    ['arts-culture','events'],
]:
    print(terms,'=>',choose(terms))
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
# Simulate picker scoring for known problematic term sets
from hashlib import md5

def pr(slug):
    p={
        'live-music':90,'music':90,'comedy':88,'theatre':86,'film':84,'food-drink':82,'food':82,
        'outdoors':80,'community':78,'sports':76,'family':74,'nightlife':72,'market':70,'markets':70,
        'arts-and-culture':20,'arts-culture':20,'arts':20,'events':10,'event':10,
    }
    return p.get(slug,50)

def gen(slug):
    return slug in {'events','event','all-events','general','misc','miscellaneous','other','uncategorized'}

def choose(slugs):
    hue_map=set(['music','live-music','comedy','arts','arts-culture','arts-and-culture','food','food-drink','outdoors','film','theatre','community','sports','family','nightlife','market','markets'])
    best=None; bests=-10**9
    for slug in slugs:
        s=pr(slug)
        if slug in hue_map: s+=20
        if gen(slug): s-=30
        if s>bests:
            bests=s; best=slug
    return best,bests

for terms in [
    ['arts-culture','live-music'],
    ['arts-culture','comedy'],
    ['events','film'],
    ['arts-culture','events'],
]:
    print(terms,'=>',choose(terms))
PY
```

```
['arts-culture', 'live-music'] =&gt; ('live-music', 110)
['arts-culture', 'comedy'] =&gt; ('comedy', 108)
['events', 'film'] =&gt; ('film', 104)
['arts-culture', 'events'] =&gt; ('arts-culture', 40)

```

```
diff --git a/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php b/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php
index ca42740..0d3394f 100644
--- a/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php
+++ b/wp-content/themes/halifax-now-broadsheet/functions.php
@@ -450,6 +450,40 @@ function hfx_event_is_generic_category_slug( $slug ) {
 	return in_array( $slug, $generic, true );
 }
 
+/**
+ * Priority score for choosing the most informative category term.
+ *
+ * Higher score wins.
+ *
+ * @param string $slug Category slug.
+ * @return int
+ */
+function hfx_event_category_priority( $slug ) {
+	$slug = sanitize_title( (string) $slug );
+	$priority = array(
+		'live-music'       =&gt; 90,
+		'music'            =&gt; 90,
+		'comedy'           =&gt; 88,
+		'theatre'          =&gt; 86,
+		'film'             =&gt; 84,
+		'food-drink'       =&gt; 82,
+		'food'             =&gt; 82,
+		'outdoors'         =&gt; 80,
+		'community'        =&gt; 78,
+		'sports'           =&gt; 76,
+		'family'           =&gt; 74,
+		'nightlife'        =&gt; 72,
+		'market'           =&gt; 70,
+		'markets'          =&gt; 70,
+		'arts-and-culture' =&gt; 20,
+		'arts-culture'     =&gt; 20,
+		'arts'             =&gt; 20,
+		'events'           =&gt; 10,
+		'event'            =&gt; 10,
+	);
+	return (int) ( $priority[ $slug ] ?? 50 );
+}
+
 /**
  * Choose the best category term for display and color seeding.
  *
@@ -494,17 +528,25 @@ function hfx_pick_event_category_term( $terms ) {
 	}
 
 	$hue_map = hfx_event_category_hue_map();
+	$best    = null;
+	$best_score = -100000;
 	foreach ( $candidates as $candidate ) {
-		if ( ! hfx_event_is_generic_category_slug( $candidate['slug'] ) &amp;&amp; isset( $hue_map[ $candidate['slug'] ] ) ) {
-			return $candidate;
+		$slug = (string) $candidate['slug'];
+		$score = hfx_event_category_priority( $slug );
+		if ( isset( $hue_map[ $slug ] ) ) {
+			$score += 20;
 		}
-	}
-	foreach ( $candidates as $candidate ) {
-		if ( ! hfx_event_is_generic_category_slug( $candidate['slug'] ) ) {
-			return $candidate;
+		if ( hfx_event_is_generic_category_slug( $slug ) ) {
+			$score -= 30;
+		}
+		if ( $score &gt; $best_score ) {
+			$best_score = $score;
+			$best = $candidate;
 		}
 	}
-
+	if ( is_array( $best ) ) {
+		return $best;
+	}
 	return $candidates[0];
 }
 

```

_**Agent (default)**_

Excellent file to share — it helped pinpoint the real issue.

You were right to flag it. The key mismatch was:

- Your handoff expects hue selection to reflect the event’s intended category signal.
- Live events often have **multiple categories** (e.g. `arts-culture` + `live-music`).
- The existing picker was too likely to choose the umbrella `arts-culture`, which kept cards in a purple family.

I implemented and pushed a fix:

- **Commit:** `b6c953d`
- **Repo:** `https://github.com/katebspurr-png/hfx-now.git`
- **Change:** category term selection now prioritizes specific categories (like `live-music`, `comedy`, `film`) over umbrella buckets (`arts-culture`, `events`) when both are present.

This should produce much closer-to-mockup color variety on the wall.

If it still looks unchanged, it’s now almost certainly deploy/cache state again. Next immediate steps:
1. deploy latest `main` (includes `b6c953d`)
2. purge full page cache/CDN
3. hard refresh

If you want, I can next add a temporary debug line that prints each wall card’s chosen category + hue so we can confirm production is using the new selector in one glance.

---

_**User**_

sure 

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Perfect — I’ll add a temporary, query-flagged debug panel on the homepage that shows each wall card’s chosen category and hue, so you can verify production behavior instantly without affecting normal visitors.
